World.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
World.desc: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

World.title: Satellite images reveal scale of Israeli demolitions as Lebanese villages destroyed
World.desc: BBC Verify analysis found more than 1,400 buildings had been destroyed since 2 March.

World.title: Frank Gardner: What is China's role in the Iran war?
World.desc: BBC Security Correspondent Frank Gardner explains how the world's second-largest economy fits into the Gulf conflict.

World.title: Trump's rift with Pope is playing out in public - it's costing him valuable support
World.desc: Leading conservative Catholics tell the BBC why they back the American pontiff in his spat with Trump.

World.title: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
World.desc: Eight students and one teacher died in the attack, according to Interior Minister Mustafa Cifci.

World.title: Former US Marine pilot loses appeal against extradition from Australia
World.desc: Daniel Duggan was arrested in 2022 over claims he illegally trained the Chinese military in South Africa.

Technology.title: Social media leaders called to Downing Street over children's safety
Technology.desc: Top executives from firms such as Meta and YouTube will be asked what they are doing to protect children.

Technology.title: Booking.com customers warned of 'reservation hijacking' after hack
Technology.desc: The travel platform said it had changed Pins to protect customers but would not say how many were affected.

Technology.title: Google to punish sites that trap people in with back button tricks
Technology.desc: The tech giant said it will punish sites that block back button navigation from June.

Technology.title: Snapchat owner cuts 1,000 jobs as says AI will reduce repetitive work
Technology.desc: The Snapchat owner is laying off around 16% of staff and withdrawn hundreds of open job roles.

Technology.title: Palantir defends its record as MPs demand more scrutiny of data use
Technology.desc: NHS guidance that all hospitals should be using Palantir software from this month has sparked a backlash.

Technology.title: Man charged with attempted murder over attack on home of OpenAI's Sam Altman
Technology.desc: The Texas man, who also faces federal felony charges, allegedly had documents advocating for violence against AI executives.

Science.title: Butterfly numbers are dropping but here are five species you may see more of
Science.desc: A warming climate has helped some to flourish, researchers say, but the outlook is troubling.

Science.title: New footage shows moment Orion capsule hatch is opened at sea
Science.desc: Newly released video shows the moment the hatch of Artemis II's Orion capsule is unlocked to a joyful reunion with the four astronauts.

Science.title: From blast off to splashdown: My days following Nasa's historic mission to the Moon
Science.desc: BBC Science Editor Rebecca Morelle reflects on how it felt to watch history being made.

Science.title: Golden eagles' return to English skies gets government backing
Science.desc: The birds could be reintroduced as early as next year following a £1m injection from the government.

Science.title: Artemis II mission was a triumph. Now comes the hard part
Science.desc: The mission was almost flawless but there are considerable obstacles ahead before a Moon landing.

Science.title: Want to help garden birds? Don't feed them in warmer months, says RSPB
Science.desc: The UK's largest bird charity has issued new guidance advising people to stop using feeders to help wildlife thrive.
